solution.answer: $H_k(S^n) \cong \mathbb{Z}$ for $k = 0$ and $k = n$, zero otherwise (confirmed by induction).
steps: Let $U = S^n \setminus \{N\} \cong \mathbb{R}^n$ and $V = S^n \setminus \{S\} \cong \mathbb{R}^n$. Then $U \cap V \simeq S^{n-1}$.
Mayer–Vietoris: $\cdots \to H_k(U \cap V) \to H_k(U) \oplus H_k(V) \to H_k(S^n) \xrightarrow{\partial} H_{k-1}(U \cap V) \to \cdots$
Since $U, V$ are contractible, $H_k(U) = H_k(V) = 0$ for $k \geq 1$. The sequence for $k \geq 2$ reduces to $0 \to H_k(S^n) \xrightarrow{\cong} H_{k-1}(S^{n-1}) \to 0$.
So $H_k(S^n) \cong H_{k-1}(S^{n-1})$ for $k \geq 2$. By induction (base case $S^1$: $H_1(S^1) = \mathbb{Z}$), $H_n(S^n) = \mathbb{Z}$.
For $k = 1$: $0 \to H_1(S^n) \to H_0(S^{n-1}) \to H_0(U) \oplus H_0(V) \to \cdots$ With $n \geq 2$, $H_0(S^{n-1}) \to \mathbb{Z} \oplus \mathbb{Z}$ is injective, giving $H_1(S^n) = 0$.
